items.title: Asian entrepreneurs face tougher climate amid geopolitical strains: ARCB
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Asian entrepreneurs are facing a more challenging business environment due to rising geopolitical tensions and ongoing global supply chain disruptions, ARCB Asia said. Udesh said ARCB, a Dubai-based venture capital and development group, views Malaysia and Asean as being strategically positioned within the transition. A regional, digital and strategic approach is now essential," he said.

items.title: Neighbours hear cries for help before college student found dead in fire
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: "I woke up when I heard a commotion outside before seeing thick smoke coming from my neighbour's house," said Nurul Huda Murshidi. The 48-year-old resident said she alerted her husband after realising the neighbour's house in Lorong Astana, Bandar Seri Astana, was on fire in the early hours of the morning, a blaze that claimed one life . "Neighbours said they heard people calling for help from inside the house, but nothing could be done as the fire spread quickly," she said.

items.title: Malaysia is expensive? Local tourism group say perception matters more than numbers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 8 — Claims on Indonesian social media suggesting Malaysia has become too expensive for tourists have raised concern among tourism industry players, though arrivals have not been significantly affected. Malaysian Association of Tour and Travel Agents president Datuk Mohd Khalid Harun said the narrative could still influence how potential visitors perceive the country, Harian Metro reported today. He said Malaysia remains a strong destination for Indonesian travellers due to cultural proximity, language familiarity, halal food options, frequent flights and family ties.

items.title: Food delivery rider fined RM2,500 for keeping stolen MyKad
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A food delivery rider was fined RM2,500 by the magistrate's court today for dishonestly retaining a stolen MyKad belonging to a hospital pharmacy staff member in March. Magistrate Farah Nabihah Muhamad Dan also ordered Muhammad Naufal Razak, 36, to serve one month in jail if he fails to pay the fine. He pleaded guilty to a charge of dishonestly retaining the stolen MyKad of a 38-year-old woman at 5pm at a pharmacy in Hospital Canselor Tuanku Mukhriz, Jalan Yaacob Latif in Cheras on March 13.

items.title: Malaysia aims to build advanced semiconductor packaging in two years
items.summary: BATU KAWAN: Malaysia aims to develop domestic capability in advanced semiconductor packaging within two years through the establishment of the Malaysia Advanced Packaging Consortium (MAPC). Science, Technology and Innovation Minister Datuk Chang Lih Kang said the consortium was formed through collaboration among five local companies and the government under a matching grant and "whole-of-nation" approach, combining public and industry efforts to build advanced packaging capabilities. He said the five companies are SkyeChip Bhd, Inari Technology Sdn Bhd, FusionAP Sdn Bhd, Pentamaster Instrumentation Sdn Bhd and NSW Automation Sdn Bhd, each contributing distinct strengths to the sector.

items.title: DBKL shuts Taman Miharja eatery over hygiene, drainage issues
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A food premises in Taman Miharja, Cheras has been ordered to close until all cleaning and improvement works are fully completed. The order followed an inspection by Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL), which found that the operator had been carrying out washing and cooking activities on the sidewalk outside the premises, while also placing buckets and oil traps in parking spaces. Earlier, a video showing parking spaces being used to wash business equipment at the food premises went viral on social media.

items.title: Queen praises Uzbekistan’s beauty, Islamic heritage and hospitality
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 8 — Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia has expressed admiration for Uzbekistan’s architectural beauty, Islamic heritage and the warm hospitality of its people during her recent visit to the Central Asian nation. In a post titled “Rahmat, Uzbekistan!” dated May 7 and shared on the Facebook page of Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar today, Raja Zarith Sofiah described the visit as a meaningful experience filled with fond memories. “For a long time, many people, including myself, only thought of travelling to Western countries.

items.title: Mercedes-Benz to recall about 144,000 US vehicles over display issues
items.summary: BERLIN: Mercedes-Benz is recalling 144,049 vehicles in the US over instrument panel display issues, the US National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) said on Friday. The recall includes certain 2024-2026 AMG GT, C-Class, E-Class, SL, CLE, and GLC vehicles. Dealers will update the infotainment control unit software at no cost, NHTSA added.

items.title: Student seriously injured in hit-and-run, police hunt for driver
items.summary: PADANG BESAR: A 17-year-old male student was seriously injured after the motorcycle he was riding collided with a car in a hit-and-run incident along Jalan Semadong-Beseri here yesterday. Padang Besar district police chief, Assistant Commissioner Mohd Shokri Abdullah, said the incident occurred at 2.30pm when the victim, a student of Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Beseri, was riding along Jalan Bukit Merah, Semadong, on his way home to Kampung Jalan Kaki Bukit. Mohd Shokri said the victim suffered internal bleeding in the head, injuries to the chin and a fractured right leg.

items.title: MBPJ activates 24-hour flood hotline as heavy rain triggers flash floods in Petaling Jaya
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, May 8 — The Petaling Jaya City Council (MBPJ) activated its disaster operations room and emergency hotline following continuous heavy rain and storm conditions today. In a Facebook statement, MBPJ urged the public to report incidents such as fallen trees, flash floods and other weather-related emergencies through its 24-hour hotline at 03-7957 4113/03-7954 2020. The adverse weather has already led to flash floods, fallen trees and traffic congestion in parts of Petaling Jaya and other parts of Selangor and Kuala Lumpur.

items.title: Global crisis: Govt focusing on strengthening economic resilience
items.summary: BATU KAWAN: The Economy Ministry is focusing on efforts to strengthen the nation's economic resilience and safeguard the supply of key input materials amidst the ongoing global uncertainty and crises. Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the ministry is adopting a multi-pronged approach, including addressing immediate needs while formulating long-term strategies to restructure the country's economy. Akmal Nasrullah said the ministry is also looking at ways to restructure the economy to better withstand current challenges while building new capabilities for the future.

items.title: Baru Bian moves to strike out PKR’s RM10m lawsuit over party exit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 8 — Ba’kelalan Assemblyman Baru Bian has filed an application to strike out a lawsuit brought against him by Parti Keadilan Rakyat (PKR), which is seeking RM10 million in damages over an alleged breach of bond conditions after he left the party following the 14th General Election (GE14) in 2018. Baru Bian is among 16 former MPs and assemblymen being sued by PKR secretary-general Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh on behalf of the party for allegedly breaching the terms of a RM10 million bond signed with PKR. Others named as defendants are Datuk Kamarudin Jaffar, Datuk Mansor Othman, Datuk Mohd Rashid Hasnon, Datuk Seri Saifuddin Abdullah and Datuk Seri R. Santhara Kumar.

items.title: Baru Bian seeks to strike out PKR's RM10mil bond lawsuit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Ba'kelalan Assemblyman Baru Bian has filed an application to strike out a lawsuit brought against him by PKR, which is seeking RM10 million in damages over an alleged breach of bond conditions after he left the party following the 14th general election (GE14) in 2018. Baru Bian is among 16 former MPs and assemblymen being sued by PKR secretary-general Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh on behalf of the party for allegedly breaching the terms of a RM10 million bond signed with PKR. Others named as defendants are Datuk Kamarudin Jaffar, Datuk Mansor Othman, Datuk Mohd Rashid Hasnon, Datuk Seri Saifuddin Abdullah and Datuk Seri R. Santhara Kumar.

items.title: Bank Negara’s international reserves at RM508.4b as of April 30
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 8 — Bank Negara Malaysia’s (BNM) international reserves stood at US$129.7 billion (about RM508.4 billion) as at April 30, 2026. BNM said the gross international reserves comprise foreign currency reserves (US$113.8 billion), International Monetary Fund (IMF) reserves position (US$1.3 billion), special drawing rights (US$5.9 billion), gold (US$6.4 billion) and other reserve assets (US$2.3 billion). Meanwhile, the central bank said its total assets amounted to RM615.55 billion, comprising mainly gold, foreign financial assets and other reserves, including special drawing rights (RM523.69 billion), Malaysian government papers (RM13.10 billion), loans and advances (RM28.92 billion), deposits with financial institutions (RM340.61 million), property and equipment (RM4.55 billion) and other assets (RM44.95 billion).

items.title: Mr DIY raises RM540mil in sukuk debut
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Mr DIY Group (M) Bhd has completed its first issuance under its RM5 billion sukuk Wakalah programme, raising a total of RM540 million. The issuance comprises RM525 million in Islamic medium-term notes (IMTN) and RM15 million in Islamic commercial papers (ICP), the retailer said in a statement. Mr DIY said proceeds from the first sukuk wakalah issuance will be used mainly to refinance existing borrowings, support working capital needs, fund capital expenditure and for general corporate purposes.

items.title: Will political funding Bill be ready before GE16? Law minister Azalina says ‘I honestly do not know’
items.summary: MELAKA, May 8 — The proposed Political Funding Bill is still under study and has yet to secure consensus from all political parties, Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said today. The de facto law minister said agreement from all parties is essential as they will be the main stakeholders and users of the proposed legislation. She said discussions on political funding are ongoing, with several key policy issues still unresolved, including public funding for parties, contribution limits and the body that will oversee political funds.

items.title: Couple jailed one week for stealing beer, powdered milk worth RM252
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A married couple was jailed for one week by the magistrate's court today for stealing a carton of beer and a tin of powdered milk worth RM252.30 from a convenience store last month. The court heard that the wife, M. Vaneesri, 43, a grandmother of two, admitted committing the offence with her husband, T. Sugumaran, 38, a foreman. The couple pleaded guilty before magistrate Farah Nabihah Muhamad Dan.

items.title: Road users advised to plan journeys ahead of KL run around Padang Merbok tomorrow
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 8 — The public is advised to plan their journeys to avoid traffic congestion during the “XTEP 10KM Time Trial 2026” running programme in the capital tomorrow. Head of the Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department (JSPT), ACP Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa, said the programme would take place from 5am to 9am around Padang Merbok. Mohd Zamzuri said the route would begin at Padang Merbok and pass through Jalan Berjasa, Jalan Sultan Salahuddin, Bulatan Dato Onn, Jalan Raja Laut, Jalan Putra, Jalan Tun Ismail, Jalan Dato Onn, Lebuhraya Sultan Iskandar (Mahameru) and Jalan Parlimen.

items.title: Heavy rain, thunderstorms expected across several states
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Heavy rain and thunderstorms are expected in several parts of the country until 6pm, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department. The department said the warning covers the whole of Perlis, Perak, Selangor, Negri Sembilan, Melaka, Johor and the Federal Territories of Kuala Lumpur, Putrajaya and Labuan. In Kedah, the affected districts are Kubang Pasu, Pokok Sena, Padang Terap, Pendang, Kuala Muda, Sik and Baling.

items.title: Queen enchanted by Uzbekistan's Islamic heritage, hospitality
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia has expressed admiration for Uzbekistan's architectural beauty, Islamic heritage and the warm hospitality of its people during her recent visit to the Central Asian nation. In a post titled "Rahmat, Uzbekistan!" dated May 7 and shared on the Facebook page of Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar today, Raja Zarith Sofiah described the visit as a meaningful experience filled with fond memories.

items.title: Ex-Armed Forces chief’s RM3m graft case: 23 documents submitted to defence
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 8 — The defence in former chief of Defence Force Tan Sri Mohd Nizam Jaffar’s case has received 23 documents, including recorded statements, in relation to four charges involving abuse of power and criminal breach of trust amounting to RM3 million. Lawyer Abdul Aziz Hamzah, representing Mohd Nizam, 59, said the additional documents were handed over by the prosecution during case mention proceedings before Sessions Court judge Rosli Ahmad today. “Today, the prosecution submitted documents related to the charges as requested by the defence under Section 51A of the Criminal Procedure Code.

items.title: #NSTviral: Teachers earn admiration for helping pupils during heavy rain [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A group of teachers has received praise online after they were seen braving heavy rain under a canvas shelter to help pupils get home safely after school. Some netizens also noticed the teachers shielding the pupils from the rain while getting drenched themselves. User @nurafaf thanked the teachers for their effort and care.

items.title: Political Funding Bill still under study, requires agreement from all parties - Azalina
items.summary: Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said such an agreement is crucial as political parties would be the primary stakeholders and users of the legislation. She said studies on political funding mechanisms are ongoing, but several policy matters have yet to reach consensus, including public funding for political parties, contribution limits and the authority responsible for regulating political funds. Some political parties also raised the issue during the 15th general election.

items.title: Storm hits Klang Valley: Flash floods, toppled trees and road congestion from Ampang to Meru and beyond (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 8 — Flash floods hit several parts of the Klang Valley this afternoon following hours of heavy rain, causing traffic congestion and disruption in Kuala Lumpur and Selangor. In the Klang Valley, heavy rain led to rising water levels in several areas, with motorists reporting slow-moving traffic and flooded roads in multiple locations on social media platforms. In an updated alert, MetMalaysia said the same stormy conditions are expected to continue till 6pm in Selangor, Kuala Lumpur, Putrajaya, Negeri Sembilan, Melaka and Johor.

items.title: PJ turns to long-term infrastructure upgrades to tackle flash floods
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: Petaling Jaya is planning long-term infrastructure upgrades to strengthen flood mitigation measures as the city continues to grapple with flash floods. The Petaling Jaya City Council (MBPJ) said it and the Irrigation and Drainage Department have outlined several structural interventions along Sungai Penchala and Sungai Klang. Yesterday's flash floods inundated parts of Petaling Jaya, including Sections 51, 17 and 19 along Sungai Penchala, as well as Taman Medan, Sri Manja and Buana Perdana near Sungai Klang.

items.title: [UPDATED] Fallen tree damages car, blocks traffic on Jalan Gombak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A car was damaged after a tree fell on it on Jalan Gombak near Batu 10 following heavy rain in the area. "A fallen tree near Batu 10, Jalan Gombak, is blocking traffic in both directions. Meanwhile, Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said the department received a call at 1.33pm.

items.title: Pahang must look beyond Genting, Camerons to diversify tourism, says Tengku Hassanal
items.summary: KUANTAN: Pahang must diversify its tourism appeal beyond Genting Highlands and Cameron Highlands by revitalising other locations such as Cherating beach. Tengku Mahkota Pahang Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah Al-Sultan Abdullah said the state has many attractive destinations, including Pulau Tioman and Cherating, that should be further promoted to holidaymakers. Tengku Hassanal urged the state Tourism Committee chairman, Tourism Pahang and all relevant agencies to step up efforts and be more proactive in attracting visitors.

items.title: Five-month-old boy killed after drug-positive lorry driver crashes into Perodua Myvi on Baling road
items.summary: BALING, May 8 — A five-month-old baby boy in Kedah was killed while his parents were injured in a collision with a lorry driven by a man who tested positive for drugs. Baling district police chief Superintendent Brandon Richard Joe said the infant was pronounced dead at Baling Hospital’s emergency ward. Police arrested the 43-year-old lorry driver after his initial urine screening returned positive for drugs.

items.title: Sabah taps new technology to boost turtle conservation
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Authorities and conservation groups are now using cutting-edge technology to collect more data to strengthen turtle conservation efforts. Sabah Wildlife Department, WWF-Malaysia and Mataking Reef Resort, which signed a memorandum of understanding (MoU) for the project, will focus on data collection on the critically endangered hawksbill turtle (Eretmochelys imbricata). The two-year project builds on long-standing conservation partnerships on Mataking Island, where the Sabah Wildlife Department, together with Mataking Reef Resort and WWF-Malaysia, recorded over 460 hawksbill turtle nests between 2009 and 2025, including 119 nests in 2025 alone.

items.title: #NSTviral: No lifts, no choice: Family treks 36 floors in Klang Valley condo
items.summary: According to the video caption, the lifts were shut down after rainwater entered the lift shaft, forcing residents to use the stairs. In the comments section, many sympathised with the children, who were seen carrying heavy school bags and struggling with the long climb. They even had to carry heavy school bags," wrote user @Zulhelmi Zaini.

items.title: Bank Negara's international reserves at US$129.7bil as at April 30
items.summary: Bank Negara said the gross international reserves comprise foreign currency reserves (US$113.8 billion), International Monetary Fund (IMF) reserves position (US$1.3 billion), special drawing rights (US$5.9 billion), gold (US$6.4 billion) and other reserve assets (US$2.3 billion). Meanwhile, the central bank said its total assets amounted to RM615.55 billion, comprising mainly gold, foreign financial assets and other reserves, including special drawing rights (RM523.69 billion), Malaysian government papers (RM13.10 billion), loans and advances (RM28.92 billion), deposits with financial institutions (RM340.61 million), property and equipment (RM4.55 billion) and other assets (RM44.95 billion). The central bank's capital stood at RM100 million, with reserves amounting to RM194.26 billion, alongside Bank Negara papers (RM7.58 billion), allocation of special drawing rights (RM26.38 billion) and other liabilities (RM3.43 billion).

items.title: Toyota halves quarterly profit as Iran crisis hits
items.summary: Toyota reported an operating profit of 569.4 billion yen (US$3.6 billion) for the three months to March 31, compared with 1.1 trillion yen a year earlier. For the year that just started, it expects an operating profit of three trillion yen. In total, the impact of the Middle East crisis will be around 670 billion yen in the current financial year, Toyota said.

items.title: Malaysia's biodiesel moment
items.summary: FOR years, Malaysia's biodiesel policy has behaved a little like a plantation road after rain - passable, promising but never entirely smooth. With B15 biodiesel production scheduled to begin from June 1 through 19 licensed plants, Malaysia is being invited to revisit a question that has never quite gone away: should palm biodiesel remain a cautious blending programme, or become a more deliberate pillar of national energy security? Malaysia's total biodiesel production capacity is about 2.4 million tonnes a year.

items.title: Road closures, diversions in Bukit Bintang for Quranic Madani event tonight
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Several roads around Jalan Bukit Bintang will be closed and diverted in stages tonight for the Quranic Madani programme, "Night Reset: Bersatu Dalam Saf @ Bukit Bintang." Kuala Lumpur Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the closures and diversions would be carried out in stages to ensure smooth traffic flow and public safety. Lee traffic police station at 03-2071 9999 or the nearest police station.

items.title: RTS Link: Real-time monitoring for all travellers; Malaysia, S'pore agree on legal jurisdiction
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Every movement through the Johor Baru–Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link will be recorded, analysed, and monitored in real time as part of a high-tech national border security framework. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the system, set to be the nation's busiest land gateway, will leverage advanced technology to balance seamless travel with stringent security and national sovereignty. With such volume, he said, strict emphasis must be placed on security, immigration control, threat detection and operational efficiency from day one.

items.title: Heavy rain triggers flash floods in parts of Klang Valley [WATCH]
items.summary: On Threads, user @tharsh.h shared that roads in Ampang were inundated following the downpour. View on Threads Another user, @imalmast , shared a similar situation in Meru, Klang. Another user, @shazwawan, shared the situation in Setia Alam.

items.title: Perak fireman in the dock with seven counts of sexual assault against 14-year-old girl in Parit
items.summary: IPOH, May 8 — A 28-year-old fireman was charged at the Sessions Court here today with seven counts of sexual offences involving a 14-year-old boarding school student in Parit, Perak. Muhammad Aidilafifi Muhammad claimed trial before judge Jean Sharmila Jesudason, several local news outlets including The Star and the New Straits Times reported. Muhammad Aidilafifi is also facing a separate charge for possessing an obscene image of the teenage girl.

items.title: Future of RM1b Jelutong landfill project in limbo after 5th EIA rejection
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The Penang government has yet to decide on the future of the RM1 billion Jelutong landfill rehabilitation and reclamation project following the fifth rejection of its Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA). State Local Government Committee chairman H'ng Mooi Lye said the matter would be brought to the state executive council (Exco) for discussion after the developer submits an extension of time (EOT) request. He said the latest EIA application was rejected by the Department of Environment (DoE) on March 2 due to unresolved technical issues.

items.title: Anwar proposes Asean regional food security plan amid global crises
items.summary: CEBU: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has proposed that Asean establish a regional food security plan as part of preparations to face global crises that could disrupt food supplies and price stability across the region. Anwar said relevant Asean ministers should immediately explore mechanisms for regional food stockpiling during times of crisis to ensure faster coordination and emergency response. "Food security must be treated as a regional strategic priority.

items.title: Let court decide on compensation for Jalan Masjid India sinkhole victim, says Hannah Yeoh
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 8 — Claims for compensation involving an Indian woman tourist who died after falling into a sinkhole at Jalan Masjid India, Kuala Lumpur, will be left to the court to decide, said Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh. She said the victim’s family could submit their claim through the courts as Kuala Lumpur had never faced such a case before. The media previously reported that the family of the woman tourist, G Vijaya Lakshmi, 48, was seeking appropriate compensation and planning to meet Hannah and Fadlun within two weeks to one month after Vijaya’s death certificate was issued yesterday.

items.title: Grab rolls out Group Ride feature nationwide as govt pushes smarter commuting
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA May 8 — Grab Malaysia today announced its new Group Ride feature that allows multiple passengers travelling along similar routes to share a single e-hailing ride and split fares. The feature enables users to arrange shared rides with friends, colleagues or family members through flexible pick-up and drop-off options. The Group Ride feature is being rolled out progressively to eligible Grab users nationwide through the mobile application.

items.title: Dzulkefly: No Malaysians on cruise ship linked to Hantavirus outbreak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Health Ministry (MoH) has confirmed that no Malaysians were on board an international cruise ship recently linked to a reported Hantavirus outbreak. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said communication with Singaporean health authorities revealed that two Singaporean crew members from the vessel tested negative for the virus. He explained that the virus presents as Hantavirus Pulmonary Syndrome (HPS) in the Americas, which primarily attacks the lungs.

items.title: Sabah reefs at high risk of coral bleaching, says Reef Check Malaysia
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Sabah's coral reefs are facing a high risk of bleaching according to a recent satellite imaging alert. Reef Check Malaysia senior programme manager Adzmin Fatta said the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) Satellite Bleaching Alert for Sabah had escalated from Watch to Warning level. "In Sabah, Reef Check Malaysia is working closely with Sabah Parks, the Sabah Fisheries Department and the Sabah Biodiversity Centre to monitor the situation closely.

items.title: Police: Man in his 80s killed in violent robbery at Ayer Tawar home, wife hospitalised
items.summary: Manjung police chief ACP Hasbullah Abd Rahman said police received a call from a member of the public at about 5.30am informing them of the robbery. Neighbours who heard the commotion came to assist the victims and took them to Seri Manjung Hospital for treatment,” he said in a statement today. He said police intelligence and investigations led to the arrest of a 27-year-old male suspect, who has seven previous criminal records, at about 7.45pm yesterday.

items.title: Court extends remand of two suspects in Ketereh college student murder by a week
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 8 — Two youths detained to assist in the investigation into the murder of a female college student, who was found with 61 stab wounds in Kampung Simah, Ketereh, on May 1, have had their remand extended for another seven days. The remand order against the two 19-year-old suspects was issued by Magistrate Rais Imran Hamid at the Magistrate’s Court here today. The two suspects had previously been remanded for seven days, which ended today, to assist investigations under Section 302.

items.title: Sabah manufacturers say ports in ‘paralysis’, could drive up prices and cost jobs if prolonged
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 8 — The Federation of Malaysian Manufacturing (FMM) Sabah Branch has slammed the worsening congestion at Sabah ports, describing the prolonged disruption as a man-made logistical crisis that is crippling the state’s industrial competitiveness and pushing businesses towards collapse. FMM Sabah chairman Liaw Hen Kong said port operations in the state had reached a “state of paralysis”, with severe disruptions to container trucking, frequent vessel omissions, massive container rollovers and prolonged delays in gate-in and gate-out activities becoming the “new normal”. “Due to port incompetence, businesses pay the price,” Liaw stressed.

items.title: Aidiladha moon sighting set for May 17
items.summary: The 29 locations nationwide include Baitul Hilal Sultan Ismail Building in Pontian, Johor; the Al-Khawarizmi Astronomy Complex in Kampung Balik Batu, Tanjung Bidara, Melaka; the Negeri Sembilan Observatory (MAINS); Baitul Hilal Bukit Melawati in Kuala Selangor, Selangor; and Baitul Hilal Bukit Jugra in Banting, Kuala Langat, Selangor. Other locations are Bukit Besar, Kuala Terengganu; Bukit Geliga, Kemaman; Pulau Perhentian, Besut; the KUSZA Observatory in Setiu, Terengganu; Bukit Peraksi, Pasir Puteh, Kelantan; and Bukit Kampong Tembeling in Kuala Krai, Kelantan. Additional locations include Menara Bangunan SEDC in Kota Bharu, Kelantan.

items.title: Shamsul Iskandar appeals after High Court rejects bid to transfer graft case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 8 — Former senior political secretary to the Prime Minister, Datuk Seri Shamsul Iskandar Mohd Akin has filed an appeal against the High Court’s decision to reject his application to transfer five corruption charges from the Sessions Court to the court. Lawyer Datuk Amer Hamzah Arshad, representing Shamsul Iskandar, informed Judge Suzana Hussin that his party had filed the application at the Court of Appeal on May 6. The court then set the case management for June 29.

items.title: Anwar urges Asean to set up food security mechanism for regional crises
items.summary: CEBU, May 8 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has proposed that the relevant Asean ministers urgently explore the establishment of a regional standby mechanism for food security during crises, including the ongoing conflict in West Asia, to enable faster coordination and emergency response efforts. Food security must therefore be treated as a regional strategic priority,” he said. We must recognise that strategic collaboration with trusted partners and regional groupings can strengthen Asean’s long-term energy security,” he said.

items.title: Police bust two international drug syndicates, seize RM12.6m worth of ganja at KLIA
items.summary: Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said seven foreigners comprising six men and one woman aged between 18 and 27 were arrested in the operation, carried out by police and Malaysia Airports Holdings Berhad auxiliary police. “Inspection found 64 plastic packets containing compressed slabs believed to be ganja flowers weighing 67.2 kilogrammes (kg), with the seizure estimated to be worth RM5.4 million. He said further checks uncovered 59 plastic packets containing compressed ganja flowers weighing 29.5 kg, with the seizure estimated to be worth RM2.4 million.

items.title: Jalan Bukit Bintang closed from midnight for Quranic Madani event at Pavilion KL
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 8 — Jalan Bukit Bintang will be closed from midnight tonight until 5am tomorrow to make way for the Quranic Madani “Night Reset: Bersatu Dalam Saf @ Bukit Bintang” programme in front of Pavilion Kuala Lumpur, aimed at ensuring the smooth running of the event and public safety. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department (JSPT) chief ACP Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the programme is scheduled to take place from 8.30pm to 11pm tonight. Lee Traffic Police Station at 03-2071 9999 or any nearby police station.

items.title: Penang exco says no misappropriation in RM500,000 fishermen voucher programme, cites document delays
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 8 — Penang state exco Datuk Rashidi Zinol has denied any misappropriation following claims by fishing equipment shop operators that they had not received payments under a RM500,000 fishermen voucher programme. The Rural Development, Agrotechnology, Food Security and Entrepreneurship Committee chairman said there were issues with payments involving various agencies, namely the Malaysian Fisheries Development Authority (LKIM), the State Economic Planning Division (BPEN) and several state financial agencies. He said six of the eight companies had submitted complete documents and would be eligible for payment first.

items.title: Penang yet to decide on RM1b Karpal Singh Drive project as developer seeks extension, says state exco
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 8 — The fate of the proposed Karpal Singh Drive reclamation project remains undecided, with the developer seeking more time to revise and resubmit its plans. The developer, PLB Engineering Berhad, will be applying to project owner, Penang Development Corporation (PDC), for an extension of time (EOT), state local government committee chairman Jason H’ng Mooi Lye said in a news conference at the State Legislative Assembly building here today. H’ng said the state is still evaluating the project following repeated setbacks in its environmental approval process.

items.title: Van driver claims trial to reckless driving charge over death of Year Two pupil in Batu Pahat
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, May 8 — A van driver pleaded not guilty in the Magistrate’s Court here today to reckless driving resulting in the death of an eight-year-old girl last Tuesday. Nordin Ithnin, 61, entered the plea before Magistrate Arun Noval Dass. According to the charge, the accused allegedly drove a white van recklessly, causing the death of Nur Aini Umairah Mohd Fareez, who was walking along the road in front of Sekolah Kebangsaan Seri Maimon, Parit Sulong, at about 12.30pm on May 5.

items.title: Police: Woman crashes car into Tumpat laundry shop after pressing wrong pedal
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 8 — A woman is believed to have accidentally pressed the accelerator while making a U-turn which caused the car to crash into a laundry shop at Pasar Besar Wakaf Bharu, Tumpat, yesterday. Tumpat district police chief ACP Mohd Khairi Shafie said the 11.20am incident involved a Honda City driven by a local woman in her 50s. He said initial investigations found that the woman was driving from Jalan Puteri Saadong towards the laundry premises before making a U-turn at the scene of the incident.

items.title: No ‘tebuk atap’ in Negeri Sembilan, says Mat Sabu as Amanah backs PKR’s Aminuddin as MB
items.summary: “No way Amanah will lead the ‘tebuk atap’ in Negeri Sembilan. The menteri besar Negeri Sembilan is currently Datuk Aminuddin. “And Amanah’s sole elected representative in Negeri Sembilan must support Datuk Aminuddin.

items.title: Malaysia to roll out MyNIISe to reduce congestion at Johor-Singapore checkpoint from 2027
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 8 — A new digital immigration system aimed at easing one of the world’s busiest land borders will be introduced at the Johor Bahru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said today. He said the system’s full rollout will coincide with the launch of the Johor Bahru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link service, which is expected to significantly improve cross-border connectivity between Johor and Singapore. “The MyNIISe system’s full rollout will coincide with the launch of the Johor Bahru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link service, which is expected to significantly improve cross-border connectivity between Johor and Singapore,” he told reporters after reviewing the progress of the Johor Bahru-Singapore RTS Link project at the ICQS at Bukit Cagar Station here today.

items.title: Saifuddin Nasution: Malaysia, Singapore to share CIQ facilities when RTS Link begins 2027
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 8 — Malaysia and Singapore will implement jointly co-located customs, immigration and quarantine (CIQ) operations once the Johor Bahru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link begins service next year, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said today. He said the arrangement will see Malaysian agencies and border security forces under the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency stationed at the Woodlands Customs Complex in Singapore. “Related agencies from Singapore will also be housed at the Bangunan Sultan Iskandar CIQ in Johor Bahru.

items.title: Home minister tells Malaysians to ‘take your time’ in switching to new MyKad
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 8 — Malaysians do not have to rush to change their MyKads when the National Registration Department (NRD) rolls out the new identification documents next month. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Malaysia has over 20 million citizens with the MyKad, adding that there is no requirement for an immediate exchange. “What we are trying to explain is that starting June 1, the new MyKad that comes with improved security features is available,” he said.

items.title: Penang records RM956b trade, strengthens role as key E&E export hub in Malaysia
items.summary: The Penang Yang di-Pertua Negri Tun Ramli Ngah Talib said Penang’s approved investments for 2025 totalled RM63.96 billion, citing information from the Malaysian Investment Development Authority. Out of the total approved investments, domestic investments amounted to RM38.85 billion while foreign investment amounted to about RM25.10 billion. He said Penang also recorded about RM956.37 billion in total trade for 2025, an increase of 17.9 per cent compared to the previous year.
